flashcards.back: A V x V 2D array where adj[i][j] = 1 if there is an edge from vertex i to vertex j, and 0 otherwise. For weighted graphs, adj[i][j] stores the weight.
An array of V linked lists. The list at index i contains all vertices adjacent to vertex i. Space complexity is O(V + E).

flashcards.front: Adjacency Matrix: edge check time vs neighbor enumeration time?
flashcards.back: Edge check: O(1) -- just look up adj[u][v]. Neighbor enumeration: O(V) -- must scan entire row.

flashcards.front: Adjacency List: edge check time vs neighbor enumeration time?
flashcards.back: Edge check: O(degree(u)) -- traverse the list of u. Neighbor enumeration: O(degree(v)) -- traverse only actual neighbors.

flashcards.front: How many list nodes total in an adjacency list for an undirected graph with E edges?
flashcards.back: 2E nodes total. Each undirected edge (u,v) appears twice: once in u's list and once in v's list.

flashcards.front: When should you use adjacency matrix over adjacency list?
flashcards.back: Use adjacency matrix for dense graphs (E close to V^2), frequent edge existence queries, and when V is small. Use adjacency list for sparse graphs and traversal-heavy algorithms.
